advantage_degradation_slope.interpretation: positive slope means the gap-head UnloggedErrorRate advantage degrades as rho moves away from the baseline fit distribution

applicability_boundary.admitted_family: Gaussian-OU toy world generated by the existing lab toy-world generator.
applicability_boundary.baseline_freeze_protocol: For each seed, distinction probes, high-energy and low-margin thresholds, and the gap head are fit only at baseline rho before fixed application to all eval rho cells.
applicability_boundary.deterministic_fallback_boundary: The canonical runner is called with use_torch=false through the imported #475 helper semantics; the experiment does not claim torch-trained representation coverage.
applicability_boundary.expected_channel_sensitivity: Observed degradation may concentrate in transition_unstable and intervention channels because rho primarily changes z_pair|z.
applicability_boundary.model: Script-private numpy gap-head evaluation over the #475 gap-ledger-head feature and metric semantics.
applicability_boundary.observation_noise_boundary: The current make_toy_batch API exposes no observation-noise parameter; this artifact does not cover observation-noise OOD.
applicability_boundary.rho_axis_boundary: The single rho axis changes the OU pair process z_pair|z and does not claim a changed z marginal.

negative_result_note: The gap-head UnloggedErrorRate advantage remains below vanilla across the predeclared rho grid relative to the baseline mean delta -0.452754; no tau, epsilon, threshold, or rho-grid tuning was applied after observing outcomes.
